== Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine |Image = [[File:Jinja ohkame.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = Ōkame Aza Wagodā 2-16, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173}} |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = 9th century (800s CE) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} copper-sheet roofing |Alternative names = Ōkame Daimyōjin, Iwashita Myōjin |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = Third Sunday of April |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Amatarashiwake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine''' (かしま あまたらしわけ じんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Ōkame, {{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}. It is one of the four shrines listed as {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Shikinaisha}} in Kurokawa District.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.tomiya.miyagi.jp/html/shuyu/index3.html|title=Tomiya Town / Tomiya Shūyū|publisher=Tomiya City|accessdate=2015-03-09}} === Overview === The shrine is believed to have been established at this location in the 9th century (the 800s CE). Within the shrine grounds stands an ancient Japanese evergreen oak (akagashi), designated as a natural monument by Miyagi Prefecture, with an estimated age of 500–600 years. The shrine occupies the highest point within {{ill|lt=Ōkameyama Forest Park|Q20043404|WD=Q20043404}}, located in the eastern part of Tomiya Town, which also features a symbolic tower and observation deck. === History === * Formerly known as Ōkame Daimyōjin and Iwashita Myōjin, the shrine was renamed Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji period|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}. * In January of Meiji 5 (1872), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. * In March of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated a shrine authorized to receive ritual offerings (heihaku). * In Meiji 42 (1909), seven shrines were jointly enshrined here. The shrine also preserves the {{ill|lt=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura|Q20044483|WD=Q20044483}}, which began in Kōka 5 (1848) and continues to the present day. * During times of severe {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=drought|UNKNOWN}}, mass {{ill|lt=rain-praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738}} ceremonies were held here by communities throughout Kurokawa District. * From July 9 for three days and nights in Kan’ei 6 (1853), the chief village headmen and residents from across the district made pilgrimages; rainfall followed on the 13th. In gratitude, the roof of the main shrine building was rethatched. * On May 23 of Genji 1 (1864), chief headmen, group leaders, and villagers from Ōkame and surrounding villages made pilgrimages; rain fell on the 24th, and the worship hall was built in thanks. * On October 5, Meiji 37 (1904), a fire caused by a votive lamp burned the shrine to the ground. * On May 27, Meiji 38 (1905), the current shrine buildings were newly constructed. * On March 11, Heisei 23 (2011), the shrine was damaged by the {{ill|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake|Q1136168|WD=Q1136168}}: the stone torii gate was destroyed, the foundation of the main hall collapsed, and the sacred turtle stone tilted diagonally. === The Akagashi Tree and Turtle Stone === * The akagashi tree is a giant tree approximately 20 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 5.8 meters. It was designated a town natural monument in Shōwa 38 (1963) and a prefectural natural monument in Heisei 18 (2006). * The Turtle Stone is a massive rock shaped like a turtle, measuring approximately 3.8 meters by 1.9 meters. Located east of the shrine buildings, it is said to be the origin of both the shrine’s alternative name “Ōkame Shrine” and the place name Ōkame. In Heisei 24 (2012), it was designated a municipal historic site. === Nearby Shrines === * {{ill|lt=Yuki Shrine|Q22120773|WD=Q22120773}} – 15 Shioda, Shidota, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tomiya City)|Q28690106|WD=Q28690106|lt=Kumano Shrine}} – 16 Shinmachi, Tomiya, Tomiya City * Ninomiya Shrine – 92 Nishinoseki Tateshita, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Yoshioka Hachiman == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine |Image = [[File:Jinja ohkame.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = Ōkame Aza Wagodā 2-16, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173}} |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = 9th century (800s CE) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} copper-sheet roofing |Alternative names = Ōkame Daimyōjin, Iwashita Myōjin |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = Third Sunday of April |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Amatarashiwake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine''' (かしま あまたらしわけ じんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Ōkame, {{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}. It is one of the four shrines listed as {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Shikinaisha}} in Kurokawa District.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.tomiya.miyagi.jp/html/shuyu/index3.html|title=Tomiya Town / Tomiya Shūyū|publisher=Tomiya City|accessdate=2015-03-09}} === Overview === The shrine is believed to have been established at this location in the 9th century (the 800s CE). Within the shrine grounds stands an ancient Japanese evergreen oak (akagashi), designated as a natural monument by Miyagi Prefecture, with an estimated age of 500–600 years. The shrine occupies the highest point within {{ill|lt=Ōkameyama Forest Park|Q20043404|WD=Q20043404}}, located in the eastern part of Tomiya Town, which also features a symbolic tower and observation deck. === History === * Formerly known as Ōkame Daimyōjin and Iwashita Myōjin, the shrine was renamed Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji period|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}. * In January of Meiji 5 (1872), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. * In March of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated a shrine authorized to receive ritual offerings (heihaku). * In Meiji 42 (1909), seven shrines were jointly enshrined here. The shrine also preserves the {{ill|lt=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura|Q20044483|WD=Q20044483}}, which began in Kōka 5 (1848) and continues to the present day. * During times of severe {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=drought|1=UNKNOWN}}, mass {{ill|lt=rain-praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738}} ceremonies were held here by communities throughout Kurokawa District. * From July 9 for three days and nights in Kan’ei 6 (1853), the chief village headmen and residents from across the district made pilgrimages; rainfall followed on the 13th. In gratitude, the roof of the main shrine building was rethatched. * On May 23 of Genji 1 (1864), chief headmen, group leaders, and villagers from Ōkame and surrounding villages made pilgrimages; rain fell on the 24th, and the worship hall was built in thanks. * On October 5, Meiji 37 (1904), a fire caused by a votive lamp burned the shrine to the ground. * On May 27, Meiji 38 (1905), the current shrine buildings were newly constructed. * On March 11, Heisei 23 (2011), the shrine was damaged by the {{ill|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake|Q1136168|WD=Q1136168}}: the stone torii gate was destroyed, the foundation of the main hall collapsed, and the sacred turtle stone tilted diagonally. === The Akagashi Tree and Turtle Stone === * The akagashi tree is a giant tree approximately 20 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 5.8 meters. It was designated a town natural monument in Shōwa 38 (1963) and a prefectural natural monument in Heisei 18 (2006). * The Turtle Stone is a massive rock shaped like a turtle, measuring approximately 3.8 meters by 1.9 meters. Located east of the shrine buildings, it is said to be the origin of both the shrine’s alternative name “Ōkame Shrine” and the place name Ōkame. In Heisei 24 (2012), it was designated a municipal historic site. === Nearby Shrines === * {{ill|lt=Yuki Shrine|Q22120773|WD=Q22120773}} – 15 Shioda, Shidota, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tomiya City)|Q28690106|WD=Q28690106|lt=Kumano Shrine}} – 16 Shinmachi, Tomiya, Tomiya City * Ninomiya Shrine – 92 Nishinoseki Tateshita, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Yoshioka Hachiman == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine |Image = [[File:Jinja ohkame.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = Ōkame Aza Wagodā 2-16, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173}} |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = 9th century (800s CE) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} copper-sheet roofing |Alternative names = Ōkame Daimyōjin, Iwashita Myōjin |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = Third Sunday of April |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Amatarashiwake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine''' (かしま あまたらしわけ じんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Ōkame, {{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}. It is one of the four shrines listed as {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Shikinaisha}} in Kurokawa District.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.tomiya.miyagi.jp/html/shuyu/index3.html|title=Tomiya Town / Tomiya Shūyū|publisher=Tomiya City|accessdate=2015-03-09}} === Overview === The shrine is believed to have been established at this location in the 9th century (the 800s CE). Within the shrine grounds stands an ancient Japanese evergreen oak (akagashi), designated as a natural monument by Miyagi Prefecture, with an estimated age of 500–600 years. The shrine occupies the highest point within {{ill|lt=Ōkameyama Forest Park|Q20043404|WD=Q20043404}}, located in the eastern part of Tomiya Town, which also features a symbolic tower and observation deck. === History === * Formerly known as Ōkame Daimyōjin and Iwashita Myōjin, the shrine was renamed Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji period|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}. * In January of Meiji 5 (1872), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. * In March of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated a shrine authorized to receive ritual offerings (heihaku). * In Meiji 42 (1909), seven shrines were jointly enshrined here. The shrine also preserves the {{ill|lt=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura|Q20044483|WD=Q20044483}}, which began in Kōka 5 (1848) and continues to the present day. * During times of severe {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=drought|1=UNKNOWN}}, mass {{ill|lt=rain-praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738}} ceremonies were held here by communities throughout Kurokawa District. * From July 9 for three days and nights in Kan’ei 6 (1853), the chief village headmen and residents from across the district made pilgrimages; rainfall followed on the 13th. In gratitude, the roof of the main shrine building was rethatched. * On May 23 of Genji 1 (1864), chief headmen, group leaders, and villagers from Ōkame and surrounding villages made pilgrimages; rain fell on the 24th, and the worship hall was built in thanks. * On October 5, Meiji 37 (1904), a fire caused by a votive lamp burned the shrine to the ground. * On May 27, Meiji 38 (1905), the current shrine buildings were newly constructed. * On March 11, Heisei 23 (2011), the shrine was damaged by the {{ill|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake|Q1136168|WD=Q1136168}}: the stone torii gate was destroyed, the foundation of the main hall collapsed, and the sacred turtle stone tilted diagonally. === The Akagashi Tree and Turtle Stone === * The akagashi tree is a giant tree approximately 20 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 5.8 meters. It was designated a town natural monument in Shōwa 38 (1963) and a prefectural natural monument in Heisei 18 (2006). * The Turtle Stone is a massive rock shaped like a turtle, measuring approximately 3.8 meters by 1.9 meters. Located east of the shrine buildings, it is said to be the origin of both the shrine’s alternative name “Ōkame Shrine” and the place name Ōkame. In Heisei 24 (2012), it was designated a municipal historic site. === Nearby Shrines === * {{ill|lt=Yuki Shrine|Q22120773|WD=Q22120773}} – 15 Shioda, Shidota, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tomiya City)|Q28690106|WD=Q28690106|lt=Kumano Shrine}} – 16 Shinmachi, Tomiya, Tomiya City * Ninomiya Shrine – 92 Nishinoseki Tateshita, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Yoshioka Hachiman == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine |Image = [[File:Jinja ohkame.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = Ōkame Aza Wagodā 2-16, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173}} |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = 9th century (800s CE) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} copper-sheet roofing |Alternative names = Ōkame Daimyōjin, Iwashita Myōjin |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = Third Sunday of April |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Amatarashiwake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine''' (かしま あまたらしわけ じんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Ōkame, {{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}. It is one of the four shrines listed as {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Shikinaisha}} in Kurokawa District.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.tomiya.miyagi.jp/html/shuyu/index3.html|title=Tomiya Town / Tomiya Shūyū|publisher=Tomiya City|accessdate=2015-03-09}} === Overview === The shrine is believed to have been established at this location in the 9th century (the 800s CE). Within the shrine grounds stands an ancient Japanese evergreen oak (akagashi), designated as a natural monument by Miyagi Prefecture, with an estimated age of 500–600 years. The shrine occupies the highest point within {{ill|lt=Ōkameyama Forest Park|Q20043404|WD=Q20043404}}, located in the eastern part of Tomiya Town, which also features a symbolic tower and observation deck. === History === * Formerly known as Ōkame Daimyōjin and Iwashita Myōjin, the shrine was renamed Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji period|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}. * In January of Meiji 5 (1872), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. * In March of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated a shrine authorized to receive ritual offerings (heihaku). * In Meiji 42 (1909), seven shrines were jointly enshrined here. The shrine also preserves the {{ill|lt=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura|Q20044483|WD=Q20044483}}, which began in Kōka 5 (1848) and continues to the present day. * During times of severe {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=drought|1=UNKNOWN}}, mass {{ill|lt=rain-praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738}} ceremonies were held here by communities throughout Kurokawa District. * From July 9 for three days and nights in Kan’ei 6 (1853), the chief village headmen and residents from across the district made pilgrimages; rainfall followed on the 13th. In gratitude, the roof of the main shrine building was rethatched. * On May 23 of Genji 1 (1864), chief headmen, group leaders, and villagers from Ōkame and surrounding villages made pilgrimages; rain fell on the 24th, and the worship hall was built in thanks. * On October 5, Meiji 37 (1904), a fire caused by a votive lamp burned the shrine to the ground. * On May 27, Meiji 38 (1905), the current shrine buildings were newly constructed. * On March 11, Heisei 23 (2011), the shrine was damaged by the {{ill|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake|Q1136168|WD=Q1136168}}: the stone torii gate was destroyed, the foundation of the main hall collapsed, and the sacred turtle stone tilted diagonally. === The Akagashi Tree and Turtle Stone === * The akagashi tree is a giant tree approximately 20 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 5.8 meters. It was designated a town natural monument in Shōwa 38 (1963) and a prefectural natural monument in Heisei 18 (2006). * The Turtle Stone is a massive rock shaped like a turtle, measuring approximately 3.8 meters by 1.9 meters. Located east of the shrine buildings, it is said to be the origin of both the shrine’s alternative name “Ōkame Shrine” and the place name Ōkame. In Heisei 24 (2012), it was designated a municipal historic site. === Nearby Shrines === * {{ill|lt=Yuki Shrine|Q22120773|WD=Q22120773}} – 15 Shioda, Shidota, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tomiya City)|Q28690106|WD=Q28690106|lt=Kumano Shrine}} – 16 Shinmachi, Tomiya, Tomiya City * Ninomiya Shrine – 92 Nishinoseki Tateshita, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Yoshioka Hachiman Shrine|Q22119988|WD=Q22119988}} – 39 Machiura, Yoshioka, {{ill|lt=Yamato Town|Q1205377|WD=Q1205377}}, {{ill|lt=Kurokawa District|Q1131782|WD=Q1131782}} * Suki Shrine – 4 Shimomiyamae, Komaba, == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine |Image = [[File:Jinja ohkame.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = Ōkame Aza Wagodā 2-16, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173}} |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = 9th century (800s CE) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} copper-sheet roofing |Alternative names = Ōkame Daimyōjin, Iwashita Myōjin |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = Third Sunday of April |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Amatarashiwake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine''' (かしま あまたらしわけ じんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Ōkame, {{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}. It is one of the four shrines listed as {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Shikinaisha}} in Kurokawa District.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.tomiya.miyagi.jp/html/shuyu/index3.html|title=Tomiya Town / Tomiya Shūyū|publisher=Tomiya City|accessdate=2015-03-09}} === Overview === The shrine is believed to have been established at this location in the 9th century (the 800s CE). Within the shrine grounds stands an ancient Japanese evergreen oak (akagashi), designated as a natural monument by Miyagi Prefecture, with an estimated age of 500–600 years. The shrine occupies the highest point within {{ill|lt=Ōkameyama Forest Park|Q20043404|WD=Q20043404}}, located in the eastern part of Tomiya Town, which also features a symbolic tower and observation deck. === History === * Formerly known as Ōkame Daimyōjin and Iwashita Myōjin, the shrine was renamed Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji period|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}. * In January of Meiji 5 (1872), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. * In March of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated a shrine authorized to receive ritual offerings (heihaku). * In Meiji 42 (1909), seven shrines were jointly enshrined here. The shrine also preserves the {{ill|lt=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura|Q20044483|WD=Q20044483}}, which began in Kōka 5 (1848) and continues to the present day. * During times of severe {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=drought|1=UNKNOWN}}, mass {{ill|lt=rain-praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738}} ceremonies were held here by communities throughout Kurokawa District. * From July 9 for three days and nights in Kan’ei 6 (1853), the chief village headmen and residents from across the district made pilgrimages; rainfall followed on the 13th. In gratitude, the roof of the main shrine building was rethatched. * On May 23 of Genji 1 (1864), chief headmen, group leaders, and villagers from Ōkame and surrounding villages made pilgrimages; rain fell on the 24th, and the worship hall was built in thanks. * On October 5, Meiji 37 (1904), a fire caused by a votive lamp burned the shrine to the ground. * On May 27, Meiji 38 (1905), the current shrine buildings were newly constructed. * On March 11, Heisei 23 (2011), the shrine was damaged by the {{ill|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake|Q1136168|WD=Q1136168}}: the stone torii gate was destroyed, the foundation of the main hall collapsed, and the sacred turtle stone tilted diagonally. === The Akagashi Tree and Turtle Stone === * The akagashi tree is a giant tree approximately 20 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 5.8 meters. It was designated a town natural monument in Shōwa 38 (1963) and a prefectural natural monument in Heisei 18 (2006). * The Turtle Stone is a massive rock shaped like a turtle, measuring approximately 3.8 meters by 1.9 meters. Located east of the shrine buildings, it is said to be the origin of both the shrine’s alternative name “Ōkame Shrine” and the place name Ōkame. In Heisei 24 (2012), it was designated a municipal historic site. === Nearby Shrines === * {{ill|lt=Yuki Shrine|Q22120773|WD=Q22120773}} – 15 Shioda, Shidota, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tomiya City)|Q28690106|WD=Q28690106|lt=Kumano Shrine}} – 16 Shinmachi, Tomiya, Tomiya City * Ninomiya Shrine – 92 Nishinoseki Tateshita, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Yoshioka Hachiman Shrine|Q22119988|WD=Q22119988}} – 39 Machiura, Yoshioka, {{ill|lt=Yamato Town|Q1205377|WD=Q1205377}}, {{ill|lt=Kurokawa District|Q1131782|WD=Q1131782}} * Suki Shrine – 4 Shimomiyamae, Komaba, {{ill|lt=Ōhira Village|Q1204681|WD=Q1204681}}, Kurokawa District * {{ill|lt=Ōhira Hachiman Shrine|Q11438808|WD=Q11438808}} – 48 Hachiman, Ōhira, Ōhira Village, Kurokawa District === Access === * 20 == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine |Image = [[File:Jinja ohkame.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = Ōkame Aza Wagodā 2-16, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173}} |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = 9th century (800s CE) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} copper-sheet roofing |Alternative names = Ōkame Daimyōjin, Iwashita Myōjin |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = Third Sunday of April |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Amatarashiwake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine''' (かしま あまたらしわけ じんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Ōkame, {{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}. It is one of the four shrines listed as {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Shikinaisha}} in Kurokawa District.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.tomiya.miyagi.jp/html/shuyu/index3.html|title=Tomiya Town / Tomiya Shūyū|publisher=Tomiya City|accessdate=2015-03-09}} === Overview === The shrine is believed to have been established at this location in the 9th century (the 800s CE). Within the shrine grounds stands an ancient Japanese evergreen oak (akagashi), designated as a natural monument by Miyagi Prefecture, with an estimated age of 500–600 years. The shrine occupies the highest point within {{ill|lt=Ōkameyama Forest Park|Q20043404|WD=Q20043404}}, located in the eastern part of Tomiya Town, which also features a symbolic tower and observation deck. === History === * Formerly known as Ōkame Daimyōjin and Iwashita Myōjin, the shrine was renamed Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji period|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}. * In January of Meiji 5 (1872), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. * In March of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated a shrine authorized to receive ritual offerings (heihaku). * In Meiji 42 (1909), seven shrines were jointly enshrined here. The shrine also preserves the {{ill|lt=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura|Q20044483|WD=Q20044483}}, which began in Kōka 5 (1848) and continues to the present day. * During times of severe {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=drought|1=UNKNOWN}}, mass {{ill|lt=rain-praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738}} ceremonies were held here by communities throughout Kurokawa District. * From July 9 for three days and nights in Kan’ei 6 (1853), the chief village headmen and residents from across the district made pilgrimages; rainfall followed on the 13th. In gratitude, the roof of the main shrine building was rethatched. * On May 23 of Genji 1 (1864), chief headmen, group leaders, and villagers from Ōkame and surrounding villages made pilgrimages; rain fell on the 24th, and the worship hall was built in thanks. * On October 5, Meiji 37 (1904), a fire caused by a votive lamp burned the shrine to the ground. * On May 27, Meiji 38 (1905), the current shrine buildings were newly constructed. * On March 11, Heisei 23 (2011), the shrine was damaged by the {{ill|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake|Q1136168|WD=Q1136168}}: the stone torii gate was destroyed, the foundation of the main hall collapsed, and the sacred turtle stone tilted diagonally. === The Akagashi Tree and Turtle Stone === * The akagashi tree is a giant tree approximately 20 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 5.8 meters. It was designated a town natural monument in Shōwa 38 (1963) and a prefectural natural monument in Heisei 18 (2006). * The Turtle Stone is a massive rock shaped like a turtle, measuring approximately 3.8 meters by 1.9 meters. Located east of the shrine buildings, it is said to be the origin of both the shrine’s alternative name “Ōkame Shrine” and the place name Ōkame. In Heisei 24 (2012), it was designated a municipal historic site. === Nearby Shrines === * {{ill|lt=Yuki Shrine|Q22120773|WD=Q22120773}} – 15 Shioda, Shidota, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tomiya City)|Q28690106|WD=Q28690106|lt=Kumano Shrine}} – 16 Shinmachi, Tomiya, Tomiya City * Ninomiya Shrine – 92 Nishinoseki Tateshita, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Yoshioka Hachiman Shrine|Q22119988|WD=Q22119988}} – 39 Machiura, Yoshioka, {{ill|lt=Yamato Town|Q1205377|WD=Q1205377}}, {{ill|lt=Kurokawa District|Q1131782|WD=Q1131782}} * Suki Shrine – 4 Shimomiyamae, Komaba, {{ill|lt=Ōhira Village|Q1204681|WD=Q1204681}}, Kurokawa District * {{ill|lt=Ōhira Hachiman Shrine|Q11438808|WD=Q11438808}} – 48 Hachiman, Ōhira, Ōhira Village, Kurokawa District === Access === * 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yamato Interchange|Q10934247|WD=Q10934247}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōhoku Expressway|Q428428|WD=Q428428}} * 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tomiya Interchange|Q16144881|WD=Q16144881}} on the {{ill|lt=Sendai Hokubu Road|Q11377788|WD=Q11377788}} * 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Izumi Interchange|Q11143029|WD=Q11143029}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōhoku Expressway|Q428428|WD=Q428428}} === Notes === {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Shidota Irrigation Tunnel|Q20044788|WD=Q20044788}} * {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarawake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110}} * {{ill|lt=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura|Q20044483|WD=Q20044483}} === External links === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310020371 Miyagi Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [https://travelog-jpn.blogspot.com/2012/01/blog-post_27.html Shrines of Miyagi: Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine and the Tilted Turtle Stone] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashima {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = 9th century (800s CE) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-sheet roofing |Alternative names = Ōkame Daimyōjin, Iwashita Myōjin |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = Third Sunday of April |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Amatarashiwake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine''' (かしま あまたらしわけ じんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Ōkame, {{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173|1=Tomiya, Miyagi}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. It is one of the four shrines listed as {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} in Kurokawa District.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.tomiya.miyagi.jp/html/shuyu/index3.html|title=Tomiya Town / Tomiya Shūyū|publisher=Tomiya City|accessdate=2015-03-09}} === Overview === The shrine is believed to have been established at this location in the 9th century (the 800s CE). Within the shrine grounds stands an ancient Japanese evergreen oak (akagashi), designated as a natural monument by Miyagi Prefecture, with an estimated age of 500–600 years. The shrine occupies the highest point within {{ill|lt=Ōkameyama Forest Park|Q20043404|WD=Q20043404|1=Ōkameyama Forest Park}}, located in the eastern part of Tomiya Town, which also features a symbolic tower and observation deck. === History === * Formerly known as Ōkame Daimyōjin and Iwashita Myōjin, the shrine was renamed Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji period|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}. * In January of Meiji 5 (1872), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In March of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated a shrine authorized to receive ritual offerings (heihaku). * In Meiji 42 (1909), seven shrines were jointly enshrined here. The shrine also preserves the {{ill|lt=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura|Q20044483|WD=Q20044483|1=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura}}, which began in Kōka 5 (1848) and continues to the present day. * During times of severe {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=drought|1=UNKNOWN}}, mass {{ill|lt=rain-praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} ceremonies were held here by communities throughout Kurokawa District. * From July 9 for three days and nights in Kan’ei 6 (1853), the chief village headmen and residents from across the district made pilgrimages; rainfall followed on the 13th. In gratitude, the roof of the main shrine building was rethatched. * On May 23 of Genji 1 (1864), chief headmen, group leaders, and villagers from Ōkame and surrounding villages made pilgrimages; rain fell on the 24th, and the worship hall was built in thanks. * On October 5, Meiji 37 (1904), a fire caused by a votive lamp burned the shrine to the ground. * On May 27, Meiji 38 (1905), the current shrine buildings were newly constructed. * On March 11, Heisei 23 (2011), the shrine was damaged by the {{ill|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake|Q1136168|WD=Q1136168|1=Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}: the stone torii gate was destroyed, the foundation of the main hall collapsed, and the sacred turtle stone tilted diagonally. === The Akagashi Tree and Turtle Stone === * The akagashi tree is a giant tree approximately 20 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 5.8 meters. It was designated a town natural monument in Shōwa 38 (1963) and a prefectural natural monument in Heisei 18 (2006). * The Turtle Stone is a massive rock shaped like a turtle, measuring approximately 3.8 meters by 1.9 meters. Located east of the shrine buildings, it is said to be the origin of both the shrine’s alternative name “Ōkame Shrine” and the place name Ōkame. In Heisei 24 (2012), it was designated a municipal historic site. === Nearby Shrines === * {{ill|lt=Yuki Shrine|Q22120773|WD=Q22120773|1=Yuki Shrine}} – 15 Shioda, Shidota, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tomiya City)|Q28690106|WD=Q28690106|lt=Kumano Shrine|1=Kumano Shrine}} – 16 Shinmachi, Tomiya, Tomiya City * Ninomiya Shrine – 92 Nishinoseki Tateshita, Tomiya City * District|Q1131782|WD=Q1131782|1=Kurokawa District, Miyagi}} * Suki Shrine – 4 Shimomiyamae, Komaba, {{ill|lt=Ōhira Village|Q1204681|WD=Q1204681|1=Ōhira, Miyagi}}, Kurokawa District * {{ill|lt=Ōhira Hachiman Shrine|Q11438808|WD=Q11438808|1=Ōhira Hachiman Shrine}} – 48 Hachiman, Ōhira, Ōhira Village, Kurokawa District === Access === * 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yamato {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = 9th century (800s CE) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-sheet roofing |Alternative names = Ōkame Daimyōjin, Iwashita Myōjin |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = Third Sunday of April |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Amatarashiwake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine''' (かしま あまたらしわけ じんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Ōkame, {{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173|1=Tomiya, Miyagi}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. It is one of the four shrines listed as {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} in Kurokawa District.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.tomiya.miyagi.jp/html/shuyu/index3.html|title=Tomiya Town / Tomiya Shūyū|publisher=Tomiya City|accessdate=2015-03-09}} === Overview === The shrine is believed to have been established at this location in the 9th century (the 800s CE). Within the shrine grounds stands an ancient Japanese evergreen oak (akagashi), designated as a natural monument by Miyagi Prefecture, with an estimated age of 500–600 years. The shrine occupies the highest point within {{ill|lt=Ōkameyama Forest Park|Q20043404|WD=Q20043404|1=Ōkameyama Forest Park}}, located in the eastern part of Tomiya Town, which also features a symbolic tower and observation deck. === History === * Formerly known as Ōkame Daimyōjin and Iwashita Myōjin, the shrine was renamed Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji period|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}. * In January of Meiji 5 (1872), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In March of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated a shrine authorized to receive ritual offerings (heihaku). * In Meiji 42 (1909), seven shrines were jointly enshrined here. The shrine also preserves the {{ill|lt=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura|Q20044483|WD=Q20044483|1=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura}}, which began in Kōka 5 (1848) and continues to the present day. * During times of severe {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=drought|1=UNKNOWN}}, mass {{ill|lt=rain-praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} ceremonies were held here by communities throughout Kurokawa District. * From July 9 for three days and nights in Kan’ei 6 (1853), the chief village headmen and residents from across the district made pilgrimages; rainfall followed on the 13th. In gratitude, the roof of the main shrine building was rethatched. * On May 23 of Genji 1 (1864), chief headmen, group leaders, and villagers from Ōkame and surrounding villages made pilgrimages; rain fell on the 24th, and the worship hall was built in thanks. * On October 5, Meiji 37 (1904), a fire caused by a votive lamp burned the shrine to the ground. * On May 27, Meiji 38 (1905), the current shrine buildings were newly constructed. * On March 11, Heisei 23 (2011), the shrine was damaged by the {{ill|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake|Q1136168|WD=Q1136168|1=Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}: the stone torii gate was destroyed, the foundation of the main hall collapsed, and the sacred turtle stone tilted diagonally. === The Akagashi Tree and Turtle Stone === * The akagashi tree is a giant tree approximately 20 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 5.8 meters. It was designated a town natural monument in Shōwa 38 (1963) and a prefectural natural monument in Heisei 18 (2006). * The Turtle Stone is a massive rock shaped like a turtle, measuring approximately 3.8 meters by 1.9 meters. Located east of the shrine buildings, it is said to be the origin of both the shrine’s alternative name “Ōkame Shrine” and the place name Ōkame. In Heisei 24 (2012), it was designated a municipal historic site. === Nearby Shrines === * {{ill|lt=Yuki Shrine|Q22120773|WD=Q22120773|1=Yuki Shrine}} – 15 Shioda, Shidota, Tomiya City * District|Q1131782|WD=Q1131782|1=Kurokawa District, Miyagi}} * Suki Shrine – 4 Shimomiyamae, Komaba, {{ill|lt=Ōhira Village|Q1204681|WD=Q1204681|1=Ōhira, Miyagi}}, Kurokawa District * {{ill|lt=Ōhira Hachiman Shrine|Q11438808|WD=Q11438808|1=Ōhira Hachiman Shrine}} – 48 Hachiman, Ōhira, Ōhira Village, Kurokawa District === Access === * 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yamato Tenashiwake Shrine|WD=Q135194316|1=Kashima Tenashiwake Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '宮城県富谷市大亀字和合田2-16', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '宮城県黒川郡大和町吉田', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:鹿島天足別神社]] {{wikidata link|Q20043641}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine |Image = [[File:Jinja ohkame.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = Ōkame Aza Wagodā 2-16, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173|1=Tomiya, Miyagi}} |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = 9th century (800s CE) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-sheet roofing |Alternative names = Ōkame Daimyōjin, Iwashita Myōjin |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = Third Sunday of April |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Amatarashiwake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine''' (かしま あまたらしわけ じんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Ōkame, {{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173|1=Tomiya, Miyagi}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. It is one of the four shrines listed as {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} in Kurokawa District.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.tomiya.miyagi.jp/html/shuyu/index3.html|title=Tomiya Town / Tomiya Shūyū|publisher=Tomiya City|accessdate=2015-03-09}} === Overview === The shrine is believed to have been established at this location in the 9th century (the 800s CE). Within the shrine grounds stands an ancient Japanese evergreen oak (akagashi), designated as a natural monument by Miyagi Prefecture, with an estimated age of 500–600 years. The shrine occupies the highest point within {{ill|lt=Ōkameyama Forest Park|Q20043404|WD=Q20043404|1=Ōkameyama Forest Park}}, located in the eastern part of Tomiya Town, which also features a symbolic tower and observation deck. === History === * Formerly known as Ōkame Daimyōjin and Iwashita Myōjin, the shrine was renamed Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji period|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}. * In January of Meiji 5 (1872), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In March of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated a shrine authorized to receive ritual offerings (heihaku). * In Meiji 42 (1909), seven shrines were jointly enshrined here. The shrine also preserves the {{ill|lt=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura|Q20044483|WD=Q20044483|1=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura}}, which began in Kōka 5 (1848) and continues to the present day. * During times of severe {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=drought|1=UNKNOWN}}, mass {{ill|lt=rain-praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} ceremonies were held here by communities throughout Kurokawa District. * From July 9 for three days and nights in Kan’ei 6 (1853), the chief village headmen and residents from across the district made pilgrimages; rainfall followed on the 13th. In gratitude, the roof of the main shrine building was rethatched. * On May 23 of Genji 1 (1864), chief headmen, group leaders, and villagers from Ōkame and surrounding villages made pilgrimages; rain fell on the 24th, and the worship hall was built in thanks. * On October 5, Meiji 37 (1904), a fire caused by a votive lamp burned the shrine to the ground. * On May 27, Meiji 38 (1905), the current shrine buildings were newly constructed. * On March 11, Heisei 23 (2011), the shrine was damaged by the {{ill|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake|Q1136168|WD=Q1136168|1=Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}: the stone torii gate was destroyed, the foundation of the main hall collapsed, and the sacred turtle stone tilted diagonally. === The Akagashi Tree and Turtle Stone === * The akagashi tree is a giant tree approximately 20 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 5.8 meters. It was designated a town natural monument in Shōwa 38 (1963) and a prefectural natural monument in Heisei 18 (2006). * The Turtle Stone is a massive rock shaped like a turtle, measuring approximately 3.8 meters by 1.9 meters. Located east of the shrine buildings, it is said to be the origin of both the shrine’s alternative name “Ōkame Shrine” and the place name Ōkame. In Heisei 24 (2012), it was designated a municipal historic site. === Nearby Shrines === * {{ill|lt=Yuki Shrine|Q22120773|WD=Q22120773|1=Yuki Shrine}} – 15 Shioda, Shidota, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tomiya City)|Q28690106|WD=Q28690106|lt=Kumano Shrine|1=Kumano Shrine}} – 16 Shinmachi, Tomiya, Tomiya City * Ninomiya Shrine – 92 Nishinoseki Tateshita, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Yoshioka Hachiman Shrine|Q22119988|WD=Q22119988|1=Hachiman Shrine}} – 39 Machiura, Yoshioka, {{ill|lt=Yamato Town|Q1205377|WD=Q1205377|1=Taiwa, Miyagi}}, {{ill|lt=Kurokawa District|Q1131782|WD=Q1131782|1=Kurokawa District, Miyagi}} * Suki Shrine – 4 Shimomiyamae, Komaba, {{ill|lt=Ōhira Village|Q1204681|WD=Q1204681|1=Ōhira, Miyagi}}, Kurokawa District * {{ill|lt=Ōhira Hachiman Shrine|Q11438808|WD=Q11438808|1=Ōhira Hachiman Shrine}} – 48 Hachiman, Ōhira, Ōhira Village, Kurokawa District === Access === * 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yamato Interchange|Q10934247|WD=Q10934247|1=Taiwa interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōhoku Expressway|Q428428|WD=Q428428|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} * 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tomiya Interchange|Q16144881|WD=Q16144881|1=Tomiya interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Sendai Hokubu Road|Q11377788|WD=Q11377788|1=Sendai-Hokubu Road}} * 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Izumi Interchange|Q11143029|WD=Q11143029|1=Izumi interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōhoku Expressway|Q428428|WD=Q428428|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} === Notes === {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Shidota Irrigation Tunnel|Q20044788|WD=Q20044788|1=Shidota Irrigation Tunnel}} * {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarawake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura|Q20044483|WD=Q20044483|1=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura}} === External links === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310020371 Miyagi Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [https://travelog-jpn.blogspot.com/2012/01/blog-post_27.html Shrines of Miyagi: Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine and the Tilted Turtle Stone] {{Shinto sidebar}} Haiden (worship hall) |Location = Ōkame Aza Wagodā 2-16, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173|1=Tomiya, Miyagi}} |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = 9th century (800s CE) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-sheet roofing |Alternative names = Ōkame Daimyōjin, Iwashita Myōjin |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = Third Sunday of April |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Amatarashiwake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine''' (かしま あまたらしわけ じんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Ōkame, {{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173|1=Tomiya, Miyagi}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. It is one of the four shrines listed as {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} in Kurokawa District.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.tomiya.miyagi.jp/html/shuyu/index3.html|title=Tomiya Town / Tomiya Shūyū|publisher=Tomiya City|accessdate=2015-03-09}} === Overview === The shrine is believed to have been established at this location in the 9th century (the 800s CE). Within the shrine grounds stands an ancient Japanese evergreen oak (akagashi), designated as a natural monument by Miyagi Prefecture, with an estimated age of 500–600 years. The shrine occupies the highest point within {{ill|lt=Ōkameyama Forest Park|Q20043404|WD=Q20043404|1=Ōkameyama Forest Park}}, located in the eastern part of Tomiya Town, which also features a symbolic tower and observation deck. === History === * Formerly known as Ōkame Daimyōjin and Iwashita Myōjin, the shrine was renamed Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji period|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}. * In January of Meiji 5 (1872), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In March of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated a shrine authorized to receive ritual offerings (heihaku). * In Meiji 42 (1909), seven shrines were jointly enshrined here. The shrine also preserves the {{ill|lt=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura|Q20044483|WD=Q20044483|1=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura}}, which began in Kōka 5 (1848) and continues to the present day. * During times of severe {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=drought|1=UNKNOWN}}, mass {{ill|lt=rain-praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} ceremonies were held here by communities throughout Kurokawa District. * From July 9 for three days and nights in Kan’ei 6 (1853), the chief village headmen and residents from across the district made pilgrimages; rainfall followed on the 13th. In gratitude, the roof of the main shrine building was rethatched. * On May 23 of Genji 1 (1864), chief headmen, group leaders, and villagers from Ōkame and surrounding villages made pilgrimages; rain fell on the 24th, and the worship hall was built in thanks. * On October 5, Meiji 37 (1904), a fire caused by a votive lamp burned the shrine to the ground. * On May 27, Meiji 38 (1905), the current shrine buildings were newly constructed. * On March 11, Heisei 23 (2011), the shrine was damaged by the {{ill|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake|Q1136168|WD=Q1136168|1=Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}: the stone torii gate was destroyed, the foundation of the main hall collapsed, and the sacred turtle stone tilted diagonally. === The Akagashi Tree and Turtle Stone === * The akagashi tree is a giant tree approximately 20 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 5.8 meters. It was designated a town natural monument in Shōwa 38 (1963) and a prefectural natural monument in Heisei 18 (2006). * The Turtle Stone is a massive rock shaped like a turtle, measuring approximately 3.8 meters by 1.9 meters. Located east of the shrine buildings, it is said to be the origin of both the shrine’s alternative name “Ōkame Shrine” and the place name Ōkame. In Heisei 24 (2012), it was designated a municipal historic site. === Nearby Shrines === * {{ill|lt=Yuki Shrine|Q22120773|WD=Q22120773|1=Yuki Shrine}} – 15 Shioda, Shidota, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tomiya City)|Q28690106|WD=Q28690106|lt=Kumano Shrine|1=Kumano Shrine}} – 16 Shinmachi, Tomiya, Tomiya City * Ninomiya Shrine – 92 Nishinoseki Tateshita, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Yoshioka Hachiman Shrine|Q22119988|WD=Q22119988|1=Hachiman Shrine}} – 39 Machiura, Yoshioka, {{ill|lt=Yamato Town|Q1205377|WD=Q1205377|1=Taiwa, Miyagi}}, {{ill|lt=Kurokawa District|Q1131782|WD=Q1131782|1=Kurokawa District, Miyagi}} * Suki Shrine – 4 Shimomiyamae, Komaba, {{ill|lt=Ōhira Village|Q1204681|WD=Q1204681|1=Ōhira, Miyagi}}, Kurokawa District * {{ill|lt=Ōhira Hachiman Shrine|Q11438808|WD=Q11438808|1=Ōhira Hachiman Shrine}} – 48 Hachiman, Ōhira, Ōhira Village, Kurokawa District === Access === * 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yamato Interchange|Q10934247|WD=Q10934247|1=Taiwa interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōhoku Expressway|Q428428|WD=Q428428|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} * 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tomiya Interchange|Q16144881|WD=Q16144881|1=Tomiya interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Sendai Hokubu Road|Q11377788|WD=Q11377788|1=Sendai-Hokubu Road}} * 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Izumi Interchange|Q11143029|WD=Q11143029|1=Izumi interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōhoku Expressway|Q428428|WD=Q428428|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} === Notes === {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Shidota Irrigation Tunnel|Q20044788|WD=Q20044788|1=Shidota Irrigation Tunnel}} * {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarawake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura|Q20044483|WD=Q20044483|1=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura}} === External links === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310020371 Miyagi Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [https://travelog-jpn.blogspot.com/2012/01/blog-post_27.html Shrines of Miyagi: Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine and the Tilted Turtle Stone] {{Shinto sidebar}} {{wikidata link|Q20043641}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine |Image = [[File:Jinja ohkame.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = Ōkame Aza Wagodā 2-16, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173|1=Tomiya, Miyagi}} |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = 9th century (800s CE) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-sheet roofing |Alternative names = Ōkame Daimyōjin, Iwashita Myōjin |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = Third Sunday of April |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Amatarashiwake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine''' (かしま あまたらしわけ じんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Ōkame, {{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173|1=Tomiya, Miyagi}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. It is one of the four shrines listed as {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} in Kurokawa District.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.tomiya.miyagi.jp/html/shuyu/index3.html|title=Tomiya Town / Tomiya Shūyū|publisher=Tomiya City|accessdate=2015-03-09}} === Overview === The shrine is believed to have been established at this location in the 9th century (the 800s CE). Within the shrine grounds stands an ancient Japanese evergreen oak (akagashi), designated as a natural monument by Miyagi Prefecture, with an estimated age of 500–600 years. The shrine occupies the highest point within {{ill|lt=Ōkameyama Forest Park|Q20043404|WD=Q20043404|1=Ōkameyama Forest Park}}, located in the eastern part of Tomiya Town, which also features a symbolic tower and observation deck. === History === * Formerly known as Ōkame Daimyōjin and Iwashita Myōjin, the shrine was renamed Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji period|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}. * In January of Meiji 5 (1872), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In March of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated a shrine authorized to receive ritual offerings (heihaku). * In Meiji 42 (1909), seven shrines were jointly enshrined here. The shrine also preserves the {{ill|lt=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura|Q20044483|WD=Q20044483|1=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura}}, which began in Kōka 5 (1848) and continues to the present day. * During times of severe {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=drought|1=UNKNOWN}}, mass {{ill|lt=rain-praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} ceremonies were held here by communities throughout Kurokawa District. * From July 9 for three days and nights in Kan’ei 6 (1853), the chief village headmen and residents from across the district made pilgrimages; rainfall followed on the 13th. In gratitude, the roof of the main shrine building was rethatched. * On May 23 of Genji 1 (1864), chief headmen, group leaders, and villagers from Ōkame and surrounding villages made pilgrimages; rain fell on the 24th, and the worship hall was built in thanks. * On October 5, Meiji 37 (1904), a fire caused by a votive lamp burned the shrine to the ground. * On May 27, Meiji 38 (1905), the current shrine buildings were newly constructed. * On March 11, Heisei 23 (2011), the shrine was damaged by the {{ill|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake|Q1136168|WD=Q1136168|1=Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}: the stone torii gate was destroyed, the foundation of the main hall collapsed, and the sacred turtle stone tilted diagonally. === The Akagashi Tree and Turtle Stone === * The akagashi tree is a giant tree approximately 20 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 5.8 meters. It was designated a town natural monument in Shōwa 38 (1963) and a prefectural natural monument in Heisei 18 (2006). * The Turtle Stone is a massive rock shaped like a turtle, measuring approximately 3.8 meters by 1.9 meters. Located east of the shrine buildings, it is said to be the origin of both the shrine’s alternative name “Ōkame Shrine” and the place name Ōkame. In Heisei 24 (2012), it was designated a municipal historic site. === Nearby Shrines === * {{ill|lt=Yuki Shrine|Q22120773|WD=Q22120773|1=Yuki Shrine}} – 15 Shioda, Shidota, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tomiya City)|Q28690106|WD=Q28690106|lt=Kumano Shrine|1=Kumano Shrine}} – 16 Shinmachi, Tomiya, Tomiya City * Ninomiya Shrine – 92 Nishinoseki Tateshita, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Yoshioka Hachiman Shrine|Q22119988|WD=Q22119988|1=Hachiman Shrine}} – 39 Machiura, Yoshioka, {{ill|lt=Yamato Town|Q1205377|WD=Q1205377|1=Taiwa, Miyagi}}, {{ill|lt=Kurokawa District|Q1131782|WD=Q1131782|1=Kurokawa District, Miyagi}} * Suki Shrine – 4 Shimomiyamae, Komaba, {{ill|lt=Ōhira Village|Q1204681|WD=Q1204681|1=Ōhira, Miyagi}}, Kurokawa District * {{ill|lt=Ōhira Hachiman Shrine|Q11438808|WD=Q11438808|1=Ōhira Hachiman Shrine}} – 48 Hachiman, Ōhira, Ōhira Village, Kurokawa District === Access === * 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yamato Interchange|Q10934247|WD=Q10934247|1=Taiwa interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōhoku Expressway|Q428428|WD=Q428428|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} * 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tomiya Interchange|Q16144881|WD=Q16144881|1=Tomiya interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Sendai Hokubu Road|Q11377788|WD=Q11377788|1=Sendai-Hokubu Road}} * 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Izumi Interchange|Q11143029|WD=Q11143029|1=Izumi interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōhoku Expressway|Q428428|WD=Q428428|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} === Notes === {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Shidota Irrigation Tunnel|Q20044788|WD=Q20044788|1=Shidota Irrigation Tunnel}} * {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarawake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura|Q20044483|WD=Q20044483|1=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura}} === External links === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310020371 Miyagi Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [https://travelog-jpn.blogspot.com/2012/01/blog-post_27.html Shrines of Miyagi: Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine and the Tilted Turtle Stone] {{Shinto sidebar}} Haiden (worship hall) |Location = Ōkame Aza Wagodā 2-16, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173|1=Tomiya, Miyagi}} |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = 9th century (800s CE) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-sheet roofing |Alternative names = Ōkame Daimyōjin, Iwashita Myōjin |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = Third Sunday of April |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Amatarashiwake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine''' (かしま あまたらしわけ じんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Ōkame, {{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173|1=Tomiya, Miyagi}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. It is one of the four shrines listed as {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} in Kurokawa District.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.tomiya.miyagi.jp/html/shuyu/index3.html|title=Tomiya Town / Tomiya Shūyū|publisher=Tomiya City|accessdate=2015-03-09}} === Overview === The shrine is believed to have been established at this location in the 9th century (the 800s CE). Within the shrine grounds stands an ancient Japanese evergreen oak (akagashi), designated as a natural monument by Miyagi Prefecture, with an estimated age of 500–600 years. The shrine occupies the highest point within {{ill|lt=Ōkameyama Forest Park|Q20043404|WD=Q20043404|1=Ōkameyama Forest Park}}, located in the eastern part of Tomiya Town, which also features a symbolic tower and observation deck. === History === * Formerly known as Ōkame Daimyōjin and Iwashita Myōjin, the shrine was renamed Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji period|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}. * In January of Meiji 5 (1872), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In March of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated a shrine authorized to receive ritual offerings (heihaku). * In Meiji 42 (1909), seven shrines were jointly enshrined here. The shrine also preserves the {{ill|lt=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura|Q20044483|WD=Q20044483|1=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura}}, which began in Kōka 5 (1848) and continues to the present day. * During times of severe {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=drought|1=UNKNOWN}}, mass {{ill|lt=rain-praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} ceremonies were held here by communities throughout Kurokawa District. * From July 9 for three days and nights in Kan’ei 6 (1853), the chief village headmen and residents from across the district made pilgrimages; rainfall followed on the 13th. In gratitude, the roof of the main shrine building was rethatched. * On May 23 of Genji 1 (1864), chief headmen, group leaders, and villagers from Ōkame and surrounding villages made pilgrimages; rain fell on the 24th, and the worship hall was built in thanks. * On October 5, Meiji 37 (1904), a fire caused by a votive lamp burned the shrine to the ground. * On May 27, Meiji 38 (1905), the current shrine buildings were newly constructed. * On March 11, Heisei 23 (2011), the shrine was damaged by the {{ill|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake|Q1136168|WD=Q1136168|1=Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}: the stone torii gate was destroyed, the foundation of the main hall collapsed, and the sacred turtle stone tilted diagonally. === The Akagashi Tree and Turtle Stone === * The akagashi tree is a giant tree approximately 20 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 5.8 meters. It was designated a town natural monument in Shōwa 38 (1963) and a prefectural natural monument in Heisei 18 (2006). * The Turtle Stone is a massive rock shaped like a turtle, measuring approximately 3.8 meters by 1.9 meters. Located east of the shrine buildings, it is said to be the origin of both the shrine’s alternative name “Ōkame Shrine” and the place name Ōkame. In Heisei 24 (2012), it was designated a municipal historic site. === Nearby Shrines === * {{ill|lt=Yuki Shrine|Q22120773|WD=Q22120773|1=Yuki Shrine}} – 15 Shioda, Shidota, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tomiya City)|Q28690106|WD=Q28690106|lt=Kumano Shrine|1=Kumano Shrine}} – 16 Shinmachi, Tomiya, Tomiya City * Ninomiya Shrine – 92 Nishinoseki Tateshita, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Yoshioka Hachiman Shrine|Q22119988|WD=Q22119988|1=Hachiman Shrine}} – 39 Machiura, Yoshioka, {{ill|lt=Yamato Town|Q1205377|WD=Q1205377|1=Taiwa, Miyagi}}, {{ill|lt=Kurokawa District|Q1131782|WD=Q1131782|1=Kurokawa District, Miyagi}} * Suki Shrine – 4 Shimomiyamae, Komaba, {{ill|lt=Ōhira Village|Q1204681|WD=Q1204681|1=Ōhira, Miyagi}}, Kurokawa District * {{ill|lt=Ōhira Hachiman Shrine|Q11438808|WD=Q11438808|1=Ōhira Hachiman Shrine}} – 48 Hachiman, Ōhira, Ōhira Village, Kurokawa District === Access === * 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yamato Interchange|Q10934247|WD=Q10934247|1=Taiwa interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōhoku Expressway|Q428428|WD=Q428428|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} * 10 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Tomiya Interchange|Q16144881|WD=Q16144881|1=Tomiya interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Sendai Hokubu Road|Q11377788|WD=Q11377788|1=Sendai-Hokubu Road}} * 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Izumi Interchange|Q11143029|WD=Q11143029|1=Izumi interchange}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōhoku Expressway|Q428428|WD=Q428428|1=Tōhoku Expressway}} === Notes === {{Help footnotes}} {{Reflist}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura|Q20044483|WD=Q20044483|1=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura}} === External links === * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310020371 Miyagi Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [https://travelog-jpn.blogspot.com/2012/01/blog-post_27.html Shrines of Miyagi: Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine and the Tilted Turtle Stone] {{Shinto sidebar}} Haiden (worship hall) |Location = Ōkame Aza Wagodā 2-16, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173|1=Tomiya, Miyagi}} |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Takemikazuchi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = 9th century (800s CE) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-sheet roofing |Alternative names = Ōkame Daimyōjin, Iwashita Myōjin |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = Third Sunday of April |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Amatarashiwake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine''' (かしま あまたらしわけ じんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Ōkame, {{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173|1=Tomiya, Miyagi}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. It is one of the four shrines listed as {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} in Kurokawa District.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.tomiya.miyagi.jp/html/shuyu/index3.html|title=Tomiya Town / Tomiya Shūyū|publisher=Tomiya City|accessdate=2015-03-09}} === Overview === The shrine is believed to have been established at this location in the 9th century (the 800s CE). Within the shrine grounds stands an ancient Japanese evergreen oak (akagashi), designated as a natural monument by Miyagi Prefecture, with an estimated age of 500–600 years. The shrine occupies the highest point within {{ill|lt=Ōkameyama Forest Park|Q20043404|WD=Q20043404|1=Ōkameyama Forest Park}}, located in the eastern part of Tomiya Town, which also features a symbolic tower and observation deck. === History === * Formerly known as Ōkame Daimyōjin and Iwashita Myōjin, the shrine was renamed Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji period|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}. * In January of Meiji 5 (1872), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In March of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated a shrine authorized to receive ritual offerings (heihaku). * In Meiji 42 (1909), seven shrines were jointly enshrined here. The shrine also preserves the {{ill|lt=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura|Q20044483|WD=Q20044483|1=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura}}, which began in Kōka 5 (1848) and continues to the present day. * During times of severe {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=drought|1=UNKNOWN}}, mass {{ill|lt=rain-praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} ceremonies were held here by communities throughout Kurokawa District. * From July 9 for three days and nights in Kan’ei 6 (1853), the chief village headmen and residents from across the district made pilgrimages; rainfall followed on the 13th. In gratitude, the roof of the main shrine building was rethatched. * On May 23 of Genji 1 (1864), chief headmen, group leaders, and villagers from Ōkame and surrounding villages made pilgrimages; rain fell on the 24th, and the worship hall was built in thanks. * On October 5, Meiji 37 (1904), a fire caused by a votive lamp burned the shrine to the ground. * On May 27, Meiji 38 (1905), the current shrine buildings were newly constructed. * On March 11, Heisei 23 (2011), the shrine was damaged by the {{ill|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake|Q1136168|WD=Q1136168|1=Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}: the stone torii gate was destroyed, the foundation of the main hall collapsed, and the sacred turtle stone tilted diagonally. === The Akagashi Tree and Turtle Stone === * The akagashi tree is a giant tree approximately 20 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 5.8 meters. It was designated a town natural monument in Shōwa 38 (1963) and a prefectural natural monument in Heisei 18 (2006). * The Turtle Stone is a massive rock shaped like a turtle, measuring approximately 3.8 meters by 1.9 meters. Located east of the shrine buildings, it is said to be the origin of both the shrine’s alternative name “Ōkame Shrine” and the place name Ōkame. In Heisei 24 (2012), it was designated a municipal historic site. === Nearby Shrines === * {{ill|lt=Yuki Shrine|Q22120773|WD=Q22120773|1=Yuki Shrine}} – 15 Shioda, Shidota, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Kumano Shrine (Tomiya City)|Q28690106|WD=Q28690106|lt=Kumano Shrine|1=Kumano Shrine}} – 16 Shinmachi, Tomiya, Tomiya City * Ninomiya Shrine – 92 Nishinoseki Tateshita, Tomiya City * {{ill|lt=Yoshioka Hachiman Shrine|Q22119988|WD=Q22119988|1=Hachiman Shrine}} – 39 Machiura, Yoshioka, {{ill|lt=Yamato Town|Q1205377|WD=Q1205377|1=Taiwa, Miyagi}}, {{ill|lt=Kurokawa District|Q1131782|WD=Q1131782|1=Kurokawa District, Miyagi}} * Suki Shrine – 4 Shimomiyamae, Komaba, {{ill|lt=Ōhira Village|Q1204681|WD=Q1204681|1=Ōhira, Miyagi}}, Kurokawa District * {{ill|lt=Ōhira Hachiman Shrine|Q11438808|WD=Q11438808|1=Ōhira Hachiman Shrine}} – 48 Hachiman, Ōhira, Ōhira Village, Kurokawa District === Access === * 20 minutes by car from {{ill|lt=Yamato {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Mikoto|1=Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = 9th century (800s CE) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-sheet roofing |Alternative names = Ōkame Daimyōjin, Iwashita Myōjin |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = Third Sunday of April |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Amatarashiwake Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine''' (かしま あまたらしわけ じんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Ōkame, {{ill|lt=Tomiya City|Q1205173|WD=Q1205173|1=Tomiya, Miyagi}}, {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}. It is one of the four shrines listed as {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} in Kurokawa District.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.town.tomiya.miyagi.jp/html/shuyu/index3.html|title=Tomiya Town / Tomiya Shūyū|publisher=Tomiya City|accessdate=2015-03-09}} === Overview === The shrine is believed to have been established at this location in the 9th century (the 800s CE). Within the shrine grounds stands an ancient Japanese evergreen oak (akagashi), designated as a natural monument by Miyagi Prefecture, with an estimated age of 500–600 years. The shrine occupies the highest point within {{ill|lt=Ōkameyama Forest Park|Q20043404|WD=Q20043404|1=Ōkameyama Forest Park}}, located in the eastern part of Tomiya Town, which also features a symbolic tower and observation deck. === History === * Formerly known as Ōkame Daimyōjin and Iwashita Myōjin, the shrine was renamed Kashima Amatarashiwake Shrine in the early {{ill|lt=Meiji period|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}. * In January of Meiji 5 (1872), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. * In March of Meiji 40 (1907), it was designated a shrine authorized to receive ritual offerings (heihaku). * In Meiji 42 (1909), seven shrines were jointly enshrined here. The shrine also preserves the {{ill|lt=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura|Q20044483|WD=Q20044483|1=Sakaki-ryū Eitai Kagura}}, which began in Kōka 5 (1848) and continues to the present day. * During times of severe {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=drought|1=UNKNOWN}}, mass {{ill|lt=rain-praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} ceremonies were held here by communities throughout Kurokawa District. * From July 9 for three days and nights in Kan’ei 6 (1853), the chief village headmen and residents from across the district made pilgrimages; rainfall followed on the 13th. In gratitude, the roof of the main shrine building was rethatched. * On May 23 of Genji 1 (1864), chief headmen, group leaders, and villagers from Ōkame and surrounding villages made pilgrimages; rain fell on the 24th, and the worship hall was built in thanks. * On October 5, Meiji 37 (1904), a fire caused by a votive lamp burned the shrine to the ground. * On May 27, Meiji 38 (1905), the current shrine buildings were newly constructed. * On March 11, Heisei 23 (2011), the shrine was damaged by the {{ill|lt=Great East Japan Earthquake|Q1136168|WD=Q1136168|1=Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}: the stone torii gate was destroyed, the foundation of the main hall collapsed, and the sacred turtle stone tilted diagonally. === The Akagashi Tree and Turtle Stone === * The akagashi tree is a giant tree approximately 20 meters tall with a trunk circumference of about 5.8 meters. It was designated a town natural monument in Shōwa 38 (1963) and a prefectural natural monument in Heisei 18 (2006). * The Turtle Stone is a massive rock shaped like a turtle, measuring approximately 3.8 meters by 1.9 meters. Located east of the shrine buildings, it is said to be the origin of both the shrine’s alternative name “Ōkame Shrine” and the place name Ōkame. In Heisei 24 (2012), it was designated a municipal historic site. === Nearby Shrines === * {{ill|lt=Yuki Shrine|Q22120773|WD=Q22120773|1=Yuki Shrine}} – 15 Shioda, Shidota, Tomiya City * District|Q1131782|WD=Q1131782|1=Kurokawa District, Miyagi}} * Suki Shrine – 4 Shimomiyamae, Komaba, {{ill|lt=Ōhira Village|Q1204681|WD=Q1204681|1=Ōhira, Miyagi}}, Kurokawa District * {{ill|lt=Ōhira
